design_philosophy.core_loop: 1v1 fighting matches with health bars, blocking, special moves, and gruesome finishing moves (Fatalities). Players progress through tournament ladder facing increasingly difficult AI opponents.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Head-to-head combat with high-risk execution of special moves and Fatalities; tension between offensive aggression and defensive positioning with chip damage on block
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Martial arts tournament competitor mastering exotic fighting techniques and performing spectacular finishing moves against a diverse roster of opponents
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Introduces 5-button control scheme with separate high/low punch and kick differentiation; pioneered 'juggling' mechanic (launching opponents and comboing while airborne); made blocking a strategic defensive option with dedicated button rather than pure evasion. SNES version notably censored violence (no blood, altered Fatalities) per Nintendo's content policies, creating platform-specific identity divergence from Genesis version

difficulty.continues_system: Players face tournament ladder with multiple 1v1 matches culminating in Endurance matches (2v1 scenarios) and boss fights; defeat ends tournament run
difficulty.checkpointing: Each tournament match is a checkpoint; health does not regenerate between Endurance match opponents (timer resets but health bar carries forward)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Difficulty controlled by opponent selection in tournament; single-player faces fixed gauntlet of 7 playable characters, Mirror Match, 3 Endurance matches, sub-boss Goro, and final Shang Tsung

progression.unlock_mechanism: Single-player tournament structure: players face playable character roster → Mirror Match → Endurance matches → Goro boss → Shang Tsung final boss. Victory unlocks progression through next opponent tier
replayability_hooks: Seven playable characters with distinct movesets create different tournament experiences
Multiplayer versus mode allows endless player-vs-player combat
Test Your Might minigame with difficulty escalation (wood → stone → steel → ruby → diamond) and bonus point scoring
Hidden mechanics: alternate Fatalities (e.g., Johnny Cage's unused super uppercut variant) discoverable through experimentation

world_building_techniques: Tournament framing device: seven island locations serve as fighting arenas (Shang Tsung's Island)
Character roster introduces martial arts archetypes and backstories through visual design
Minigame 'Test Your Might' serves as narrative break/training montage between fights
Lore exposition: intro explains Shang Tsung banished 500 years ago, Goro's 500-year undefeated streak, tournament's stakes for Earthrealm

boss_design.philosophy: Escalating difficulty through special boss mechanics: Goro as sub-boss introduces multi-phase combat challenge; Shang Tsung as final boss combines high AI difficulty with thematic importance as main antagonist. Endurance matches (2v1) serve as structural difficulty spike before boss gauntlet
notable_bosses: Goro (sub-boss): Undefeated fighter for 500 years; monster character with unique moveset; positioned before final boss
Shang Tsung (final boss): Primary antagonist; sorcerer controlling tournament; endgame encounter determining realm's fate

faction_indicators: Earthrealm vs. Outworld implied conflict (tournament stakes)
Tournament participants represent different martial arts traditions and cultures
Shang Tsung's tyrannical control of Mortal Kombat tournament as oppressive system
danger_signals: Censorship of violence and Fatalities on SNES (vs. uncensored Genesis version) signals platform-holder power dynamics
Shang Tsung's dark sorcery and realm-threatening ambitions
environmental_storytelling: Seven island locations with distinct arena aesthetics establish world geography
Character sprite design and movesets reflect cultural/martial arts backgrounds
Fatalities performed on defeated opponents reinforce high-stakes tournament lethality

sources_used: tcrf: Mortal Kombat (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor (technical data, censorship notes, unused content)
wikipedia: Mortal Kombat (1992 video game) (gameplay mechanics, narrative structure, development history)
